title: Heptapleurum myrianthellum

The species Heptapleurum myrianthellum is native to tropical regions of Asia and is characterized by its unique compound leaves. Its classification also places it within the order Apiales, a group of flowering plants that includes other members of the Araliaceae family.

intro: Heptapleurum myrianthellum, also known as Myrianthes or False Blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae, native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
